<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./komodo-setup.sh`

```text
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	set -euo pipefail
3	
4	# Komodo-compatible setup script for mailcow
5	# This script initializes mailcow.conf from the template with generated secrets.
6	# Run once on initial deployment. Subsequent deploys (via Komodo webhook) only
7	# need `docker compose up -d` which Komodo handles automatically.
8	
9	SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]:-$0}")" >/dev/null 2>&1 && pwd)"
10	cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"
11	
12	echo "=== mailcow Komodo Setup for mail.lgucalapan.ph ==="
13	
14	# Check if mailcow.conf already exists
15	if [ -f mailcow.conf ]; then
16	  echo "mailcow.conf already exists."
17	  read -r -p "Overwrite? This will back up the current config. [y/N] " response
18	  case $response in
19	    [yY][eE][sS]|[yY])
20	      cp mailcow.conf "mailcow.conf.bak.$(date +%s)"
21	      echo "Backed up existing config."
22	      ;;
23	    *)
24	      echo "Keeping existing config. Exiting."
25	      exit 0
26	      ;;
27	  esac
28	fi
29	
30	# Generate random secrets
31	gen_secret() {
32	  LC_ALL=C </dev/urandom tr -dc A-Za-z0-9 2>/dev/null | head -c "${1:-28}"
33	}
34	
35	DBPASS=$(gen_secret 28)
36	DBROOT=$(gen_secret 28)
37	REDISPASS=$(gen_secret 28)
38	SOGO_KEY=$(gen_secret 16)
39	
40	echo "Generating mailcow.conf from template..."
41	
42	sed \
43	  -e "s/__DBPASS__/${DBPASS}/" \
44	  -e "s/__DBROOT__/${DBROOT}/" \
45	  -e "s/__REDISPASS__/${REDISPASS}/" \
46	  -e "s/__SOGO_KEY__/${SOGO_KEY}/" \
47	  mailcow.conf.template > mailcow.conf
48	
49	chmod 600 mailcow.conf
50	
51	# Create .env symlink if not present
52	if [ ! -L .env ]; then
53	  ln -sf mailcow.conf .env
54	  echo "Created .env -> mailcow.conf symlink."
55	fi
56	
57	# Create required directories
58	mkdir -p data/assets/ssl
59	
60	# Generate snake-oil certificate if no cert exists
61	if [ ! -f data/assets/ssl/cert.pem ]; then
62	  echo "Generating snake-oil SSL certificate..."
63	  openssl req -x509 -newkey rsa:4096 \
64	    -keyout data/assets/ssl/key.pem \
65	    -out data/assets/ssl/cert.pem \
66	    -days 365 \
67	    -subj "/C=PH/ST=Calapan/L=Calapan/O=lgucalapan/OU=mail/CN=mail.lgucalapan.ph" \
68	    -sha256 -nodes 2>/dev/null
69	  echo "Snake-oil certificate generated. Let's Encrypt will replace it automatically."
70	fi
71	
72	# Create rspamd password placeholder if missing
73	[ ! -f ./data/conf/rspamd/override.d/worker-controller-password.inc ] && \
74	  echo '# Placeholder' > ./data/conf/rspamd/override.d/worker-controller-password.inc
75	
76	# Set app_info
77	mailcow_git_version=$(git describe --tags "$(git rev-list --tags --max-count=1)" 2>/dev/null || git rev-parse --short HEAD)
78	mailcow_git_commit=$(git rev-parse HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
79	mailcow_git_commit_date=$(git log -1 --format=%ci 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
80	git_branch=$(git rev-parse --abbrev-ref HEAD 2>/dev/null || echo "master")
81	
82	cat > data/web/inc/app_info.inc.php << APPEOF
83	<?php
84	  \$MAILCOW_GIT_VERSION="${mailcow_git_version}";
85	  \$MAILCOW_LAST_GIT_VERSION="";
86	  \$MAILCOW_GIT_OWNER="bonitobonita24";
87	  \$MAILCOW_GIT_REPO="mail_lgucalapan_ph";
88	  \$MAILCOW_GIT_URL="https://github.com/bonitobonita24/mail_lgucalapan_ph";
89	  \$MAILCOW_GIT_COMMIT="${mailcow_git_commit}";
90	  \$MAILCOW_GIT_COMMIT_DATE="${mailcow_git_commit_date}";
91	  \$MAILCOW_BRANCH="${git_branch}";
92	  \$MAILCOW_UPDATEDAT=$(date +%s);
93	?>
94	APPEOF
95	
96	echo ""
97	echo "=== Setup Complete ==="
98	echo ""
99	echo "  Hostname: mail.lgucalapan.ph"
100	echo "  Timezone: Asia/Manila"
101	echo "  Config:   mailcow.conf (chmod 600)"
102	echo ""
103	echo "Next steps:"
104	echo "  1. docker compose pull"
105	echo "  2. docker compose up -d"
106	echo "  3. Access https://mail.lgucalapan.ph"
107	echo "  4. Login: admin / moohoo (change immediately!)"
108	echo ""
109	echo "DNS records required:"
110	echo "  A/AAAA  mail.lgucalapan.ph          -> your server IP"
111	echo "  MX      lgucalapan.ph               -> mail.lgucalapan.ph (priority 10)"
112	echo "  TXT     lgucalapan.ph               -> v=spf1 mx a -all"
113	echo "  TXT     _dmarc.lgucalapan.ph        -> v=DMARC1; p=reject; rua=mailto:admin@lgucalapan.ph"
114	echo "  TXT     dkim._domainkey.lgucalapan.ph -> (get from mailcow admin UI after first start)"
115	echo "  CNAME   autodiscover.lgucalapan.ph  -> mail.lgucalapan.ph"
116	echo "  CNAME   autoconfig.lgucalapan.ph    -> mail.lgucalapan.ph"
117	echo "  SRV     _autodiscover._tcp.lgucalapan.ph -> 0 1 443 mail.lgucalapan.ph"
118
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:29:29Z)**_

The problem is `set -euo pipefail` combined with the `gen_secret()` function on line 32. When `head -c 28` gets enough bytes it closes the pipe, causing `tr` to get a `SIGPIPE` / non-zero exit — and `pipefail` treats that as a failure, silently killing the script.

The trace stops after the echo on line 12 because `mailcow.conf` doesn't exist (skips the `if` block), then hits line 35 `DBPASS=$(gen_secret 28)` which fails due to the broken pipe.

Here's the fix:

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:29:31Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Edit"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Edit**</summary>

```text
The file /home/me/UbuntuDevFiles/lgucalapan_ph/mail_lgucalapan_ph/komodo-setup.sh has been updated successfully.
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:29:36Z)**_

Also need to fix line 73 — the rspamd directory likely doesn't exist yet, which would fail silently or error out:

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:29:38Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Edit"><details>

</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-12 16:29:50Z)**_

**Summary of fixes:**

1. **`gen_secret()` — the actual crash** — `tr | head` triggers SIGPIPE when `head` closes early. With `pipefail`, that's a fatal error. Added `|| true` to suppress it.
2. **Missing rspamd directory** — added `mkdir -p` before writing the placeholder file.
3. **Missing `data/web/inc/` directory** — added `mkdir -p` before writing `app_info.inc.php`.

Push this and re-run the script on your server. It should get past the secret generation now.
